questions.question: Define environmental pollution and list any four major types.
keyPoints: Definition of environmental pollution as contamination of natural environment
List four types: Air, Water, Soil, Noise pollution

questions.question: Explain Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) and its significance in measuring water pollution.
keyPoints: BOD definition and measurement
Higher BOD indicates more organic pollution
Used to assess water quality and treatment efficiency

questions.question: What is acid rain? List two primary pollutants responsible for it.
keyPoints: Definition of acid rain and its effects
Sulfur dioxide (SO2) and Nitrogen oxides (NOx) as primary causes
Formation mechanism explained

questions.question: Describe the process of biomagnification with an example.
keyPoints: Definition of biomagnification
Example: DDT accumulation in food chain
How toxins increase at each trophic level

questions.question: What is E-waste? Mention two health hazards caused by improper E-waste disposal.
keyPoints: Definition of electronic waste
Health hazards: lead poisoning, neurological damage, cancer risks
Environmental impact

questions.question: Explain thermal pollution and suggest one method to control it.
keyPoints: Definition of thermal pollution
Causes: industrial cooling water, power plants
Control method: cooling towers, thermal reservoirs

questions.question: What is the Air Quality Index (AQI)? Mention any two pollutants monitored under AQI.
keyPoints: AQI definition as air quality measure
Two pollutants: PM2.5, PM10, SO2, NO2, O3, CO
Scale and health implications

questions.question: Explain the major sources of air pollution and their effects on human health and environment.
keyPoints: Sources: vehicle emissions, industrial smoke, burning fossil fuels, agricultural activities
Health effects: respiratory diseases, cardiovascular problems, lung cancer
Environmental effects: acid rain, global warming, ozone depletion
Specific pollutants and their impacts

questions.question: Discuss the causes, effects, and control measures of water pollution.
keyPoints: Causes: industrial effluents, sewage, agricultural runoff, oil spills
Effects: water-borne diseases, aquatic life destruction, biomagnification
Control measures: treatment plants, laws, public awareness, industrial responsibility

questions.question: What is soil pollution? Discuss its causes, effects on agriculture, and preventive measures.
keyPoints: Definition of soil pollution
Causes: industrial waste, pesticides, garbage dumping, heavy metals
Effects: reduced crop yield, contaminated food, groundwater pollution
Preventive measures: proper waste disposal, organic farming, bioremediation

questions.question: Explain the concept of eutrophication. How does it affect aquatic ecosystems? Suggest control measures.
keyPoints: Eutrophication definition and process
Causes: excess nitrogen and phosphorus from fertilizers
Effects: algae bloom, oxygen depletion, fish death, ecosystem imbalance
Control measures: reduce fertilizer use, wetland protection, treatment
